question.message: Would it be stupid for me to pause my 401k contributions for a few months to build up cash reserves?

trials.answer: Pausing your 401(k) contributions is a costly way to build cash because you would miss out on your employer match, which is essentially a 100% return on your money. A better approach is to reduce your contributions to the level of your employer's match, or find savings elsewhere in your budget.

Pausing your 401(k) contributions would be a costly way to build cash, especially since you'd miss out on tax advantages and potential employer matching funds. A better option is to temporarily redirect the $1,780 you contribute to your taxable brokerage account every two weeks into a high-yield savings account.
